body.title: Ground Loop collapse design — T-collapse-daemons-to-ground-loop (map + chosen approach)
body.text:
# Ground Loop collapse — design (for T-collapse-daemons-to-ground-loop)

CORRECTED 2026-07-08 (post-compact). The earlier version of this note framed the Ground Loop as a 'plugin-host process supervisor' with 'two hosting modes' and picked an 'Option B: runme.py run as a GL thread'. THAT FRAMING IS WRONG and Akien has corrected it repeatedly. See memories feedback_ground_loop_is_passive_shim_owns_startup and feedback_cooperative_not_hierarchical.

## THE MODEL (do not re-confabulate this)
The Ground Loop is PASSIVE. It does exactly two things: (1) call a shim's heartbeat if the shim wants one; (2) call rescueclaude with an error packet on a major startup bork. It supervises NOTHING. It hosts NOTHING. There is no registering, no roster, no 'the GL runs the device'.

The system is COOPERATIVE + encapsulated: each device is responsible for ITSELF. Device startup lives in the device's SHIM, triggered off the heartbeat. A device's periodic work runs as a SHIM-STARTED BACKGROUND THREAD. Canonical reference: devices/aider/ — AiderShim.start() builds AiderWorkerListener and calls listener.start(); AiderWorkerListener spawns threading.Thread(target=_run, daemon=True) running `while not stop.is_set(): _poll_once(); stop.wait(interval)`. No __main__, no subprocess, no GL hosting.

## What 'ONE daemon structure' means (Akien HARD RULE, 2026-07-08)
The Ground Loop is the ONE always-alive heartbeat/recovery loop — the single `while True` process on the box. 'No other daemons, PERIOD' = NO device runs its own standalone daemon (own `while True` + `__main__` + PID file + signal handlers + tmux/subprocess). A device's loop is a thread its SHIM owns, cooperatively driven off the heartbeat. Currently VIOLATED by granny and scraps (both hand-roll standalone daemons).

## The violation, concretely
- Granny: granny/daemon.py run_loop() = `while True: run_once(config, imap); _wait_for_work(60s)` (~daemon.py:1189-1210), plus __main__ (~1213-1234) writing ~/.granny/daemon.pid + SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers + run_loop(). Launched by ./granny -> tmux session 'granny' running `python -m ...granny.daemon`. GrannyShim currently only runs a 30s watchdog thread that checks the PID and re-launches via tmux send-keys when dead. Per-cycle body = run_once(config, *, imap=None) (~daemon.py:897). Granny keeps a persistent imap/bus handle + reloads config every cycle.
- Scraps: scraps/daemon.py run_loop = `while True: run_once(); sleep(30)` (~scraps/daemon.py:56-70), __main__ writes ~/.unseen_university/scraps/daemon.pid + signals. Launched as a GL PluginDaemon SUBPROCESS via config/ground_loop/scraps.yaml (mode: daemon). run_once ticks JOBS (StaleChatLogBackfiller). KEY: scraps already runs as a GL-supervised subprocess and Akien STILL named it a violation -> a subprocess with its own run_loop is NOT acceptable. The target is an in-process, shim-owned thread — NOT a GL-spawned subprocess.

## THE DESIGN (shim owns the loop; GL untouched)
Move each device's loop into its OWN SHIM as a background thread, aider-style. The Ground Loop is NOT modified — it already provides heartbeat + rescue.

BRING-UP IS ALREADY SOLVED — granny is demand-started by her shim OFF THE QUEUE (Akien, confirmed 4x; memory feedback_granny_shim_startup). Work lands in the queue -> GrannyShim sees pending sprint tickets (_has_pending_tickets() -> ticket_store.list(status_filter='sprint')) -> the shim starts her. This is NOT an open bring-up question. The shim ALREADY owns the trigger (its watchdog/_restart_daemon path); the collapse only swaps WHAT it starts: an in-process loop thread instead of a tmux subprocess.

For granny (and symmetrically scraps):
1. The shim's existing queue-watch (currently _watchdog_loop -> _restart_daemon) starts the loop when work is pending. Replace the tmux-subprocess launch in _restart_daemon with: build the persistent bus/imap handle (granny's _make_imap_if_bus_configured) and start a background thread — a small GrannyDispatchLoop modeled on AiderWorkerListener: threading.Thread(daemon=True) running `while not stop.wait(interval): run_once(config, imap)`, config reloaded per tick as today. stop()/join on shim.stop(). The shim owns start/stop/restart; self_test checks the thread liveness instead of a PID file; if the thread dies with work pending, the shim restarts it (no PID, no tmux).
2. DELETE from both daemon.py: run_loop(), the `__main__` block, the PID file, signal handlers, _wait_for_work. KEEP run_once(), _load_config(), granny's _make_imap_if_bus_configured() — run_once stays the per-tick body, called by the shim thread.
3. RELOCATE granny's dispatch-health emit (every N ticks) into the shim's loop so the T-granny-dispatch-observability-gap WARN survives.
4. GrannyShim: drop the tmux-relaunch watchdog (no external process to watch). Retire the ./granny launcher and granny device.py launch_command (no more tmux daemon).
5. Scraps: same shape — delete run_loop/__main__/PID/signals; ScrapsShim.start() spawns the JOBS-tick thread; retire scraps.yaml PluginDaemon descriptor.
6. LOGGING: with the loop now a thread in the shim's own DiagnosticBase device process, the canonical per-device JSON sink is already installed by the device — so the transitional configure_process_logging bridge added for the standalone __main__ (base.py) can be REMOVED once thread logs are confirmed reaching ~/.unseen_university/logs/granny|scraps/. VERIFY the device_id binding carries onto the thread's stdlib records before deleting the bridge.

DO NOT use runme.py / RunmeSupervisor / PluginDaemon for this. Those are the GL-hosts-the-thread shape, which is the hierarchical model the memories steer away from. The shim hosts the loop. (RunmeSupervisor/PluginDaemon code exists and hosts other things today; leave it alone — this ticket does not adopt it and does not rip it out.)

## Scope boundary
Collapse STRUCTURE only (where the loop lives + who owns startup). Do NOT change run_once dispatch/scrap LOGIC. The Ground Loop daemon is NOT edited by this ticket.

## Build-time checks
- Bring-up is SETTLED: the shim starts granny off the queue (see above). No open bring-up gap.
- Confirm the shim-thread's stdlib logs reach the canonical granny/scraps JSON sink (device_id stamped) before removing configure_process_logging.
- The two risky moves to advisor-check the IMPLEMENTATION of (not the model): (a) relocating granny's persistent bus-handle + per-cycle config reload into the shim thread, and (b) replacing the standalone-process logging bridge with device-process thread logging.
- On runme.py/RunmeSupervisor: the authoritative memories (feedback_ground_loop_is_passive_shim_owns_startup, feedback_cooperative_not_hierarchical) put the loop in the SHIM, not GL-hosted — and the queue-trigger confirms the shim owns granny's start. So use the shim path. Do not rip out existing runme/PluginDaemon code that hosts OTHER things; this ticket just doesn't adopt it for granny/scraps.
